title: Establish a security baseline
description: Create and maintain a comprehensive security baseline for all workload components aligned with organizational compliance requirements and industry standards such as CIS, NIST, and ISO 27001.

title: Maintain a secure software supply chain
description: Harden the CI/CD pipeline and validate code origins by applying static analysis, dependency scanning, and software composition analysis to detect vulnerabilities before they reach production.

title: Classify and protect sensitive data
description: Identify, classify, and protect sensitive data in transit and at rest. Enforce encryption using platform-managed or customer-managed keys and apply data loss prevention controls.

title: Segment networks
description: Apply network segmentation strategies to contain blast radius, reduce lateral movement, and enforce least-privilege traffic flows using NSGs, Azure Firewall, and private endpoints.

title: Use identity as the primary access control boundary
description: Implement strong identity management using Microsoft Entra ID. Apply least-privilege RBAC, use managed identities to eliminate credential management, and enforce conditional access policies.

title: Establish application-to-platform responsibility boundaries
description: Define clear security ownership boundaries between the application layer and the underlying Azure platform. Understand the shared responsibility model and harden each tier appropriately.

title: Protect application secrets
description: Store and rotate secrets, keys, and certificates using Azure Key Vault. Prevent credentials from being embedded in source code or configuration files and use managed identities to access secrets.

title: Harden development processes
description: Apply secure coding standards, mandatory code reviews, SAST/DAST tooling, and security gates throughout the development lifecycle to prevent vulnerabilities from entering the codebase.

title: Test for security vulnerabilities
description: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments, automated dependency scanning, and container image scanning. Track, prioritize, and remediate findings systematically based on risk severity.

title: Conduct regular adversarial testing
description: Perform threat modeling, penetration testing, and red team exercises to validate security controls against realistic attacker tactics. Remediate findings and retest to confirm closure.

title: Monitor security health
description: Implement continuous security monitoring using Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Microsoft Sentinel, and Azure Monitor. Configure alerts for anomalous behavior, policy violations, and security incidents.

title: Define and test business continuity and disaster recovery procedures
description: Design security-aware business continuity and disaster recovery plans. Test incident response, data recovery, and failover procedures regularly to ensure organizational resilience.

title: Define reliability targets
description: Establish measurable reliability targets including SLAs, SLOs, SLIs, RTO, and RPO that align with business requirements. Use these targets to drive all architectural trade-off decisions.

title: Design for redundancy
description: Eliminate single points of failure by deploying workload components across multiple instances, availability zones, and regions. Use Azure Availability Zones and zone-redundant services.

title: Design for business continuity across regions
description: Architect for geographic redundancy and automated regional failover. Use geo-replication, paired regions, and multi-region active-active or active-passive deployment patterns for critical workloads.

title: Design for graceful degradation
description: Implement resilience patterns such as circuit breakers, bulkheads, timeouts, and fallbacks to allow the workload to continue serving requests in a degraded but functional state when dependencies fail.

title: Add observability to reliability
description: Instrument workloads with health probes, structured logs, metrics, and distributed traces to detect reliability degradation early and trigger automated recovery before user impact occurs.

title: Design for reliability through loose coupling
description: Use asynchronous messaging, event-driven architecture, and queue-based load leveling to decouple workload components and prevent cascading failures from propagating across service boundaries.

title: Design for self-preservation
description: Implement self-healing capabilities including automatic restarts, health-triggered failovers, and durable storage tiers for stateful components. Use premium SKUs where reliability SLAs demand it.

title: Design for backup and recovery
description: Implement comprehensive backup strategies with clearly defined retention policies. Test restore procedures regularly to validate that recovery time and point objectives can be met under failure conditions.

title: Test for reliability
description: Conduct chaos engineering experiments, failure injection testing, and disaster recovery drills to validate reliability assumptions and continuously improve workload resilience under real failure conditions.

title: Establish development standards
description: Define and enforce coding standards, naming conventions, resource tagging policies, and documentation requirements. Use Azure Policy and governance tooling to ensure consistent compliance across the platform.

title: Implement supply chain traceability
description: Track code provenance, artifact versions, and deployment lineage from commit to production. Use immutable artifacts, signed containers, and a complete audit trail linking changes to deployments.

title: Formalize software ideation and planning management
description: Use structured sprint planning, backlog grooming, and requirements management. Track work items end-to-end and establish clear definitions of done to ensure predictable delivery.

title: Use continuous integration
description: Implement automated CI pipelines that build, test, and validate every code change. Enforce automated testing gates and code quality checks to prevent regressions from reaching production.

title: Standardize tools and processes
description: Standardize on a shared set of tools, platforms, and operational processes across teams. Reduce toolchain fragmentation to improve consistency, reduce cognitive load, and simplify onboarding.

title: Develop a safe deployment strategy
description: Implement progressive deployment strategies including blue-green, canary, and feature flags. Use resource locks, automated rollback, and deployment gates to prevent uncontrolled configuration drift.

title: Design and implement a monitoring system
description: Implement comprehensive observability across all workload tiers. Collect logs, metrics, and traces using Azure Monitor, Application Insights, and Log Analytics. Define actionable alerting thresholds.

title: Implement a change management process
description: Define formal processes for planning, reviewing, approving, and auditing infrastructure and configuration changes. Use infrastructure as code with pull-request reviews and policy-driven guardrails.

title: Automate build and deployment
description: Use CI/CD pipelines to fully automate the build, test, and deployment lifecycle. Eliminate manual deployment steps that introduce human error risk and create unpredictable delivery cadences.

title: Implement operational dashboards
description: Build dashboards that surface workload health, SLO compliance, error rates, and key performance indicators. Make operational health visible to all stakeholders using Azure Monitor Workbooks and Grafana.

title: Safely deploy workloads
description: Implement deployment validation, smoke testing, and staged rollouts using deployment slots or traffic splitting. Validate health before completing deployments and automate rollback on failure.

title: Automate and systematically manage workload operations
description: Automate operational tasks including patching, configuration enforcement, backup verification, and health remediation. Use Azure Automation, Update Manager, and Azure Policy to eliminate operational toil.

title: Define and communicate performance targets
description: Establish measurable performance requirements and SLOs including latency percentiles, throughput targets, and error budgets. Align performance targets with user expectations and business requirements.

title: Conduct capacity planning
description: Forecast workload capacity requirements using historical usage data, expected growth, and peak demand projections. Plan for headroom and use Azure Capacity Reservations for predictable workloads.

title: Select the right services
description: Choose Azure services whose performance characteristics match the workload requirements. Prefer purpose-built managed services over general-purpose compute to leverage built-in performance optimizations.

title: Collect performance data
description: Instrument workloads to continuously collect granular performance metrics, request traces, dependency call durations, and resource utilization data using Application Insights and Azure Monitor.

title: Optimize scaling and partitioning
description: Design workloads to scale horizontally. Use VMSS autoscale policies, App Service autoscale, and partitioning strategies to distribute load, prevent hotspots, and maintain performance under variable demand.

title: Test performance
description: Conduct regular performance and load testing to validate that workloads meet SLOs under expected and peak conditions. Use Azure Load Testing to simulate realistic traffic patterns.

title: Optimize code and infrastructure
description: Profile and optimize code hotpaths to reduce CPU and memory overhead. Enable accelerated networking, use premium storage for I/O-intensive workloads, and eliminate unnecessary compute overhead.

title: Optimize data stores
description: Select storage tiers and indexing strategies that match data access patterns. Use connection pooling, read replicas, elastic pools, and query optimization to maximize data store throughput.

title: Identify and act on bottlenecks
description: Continuously profile workloads to detect performance bottlenecks using distributed traces and profiling tools. Prioritize optimization of the critical path to achieve the largest performance gains.

title: Optimize flow of workload data
description: Minimize data transfer latency by using content delivery networks, response compression, efficient serialization formats, and traffic routing policies that direct users to the nearest service instance.

title: Optimize with a continuous improvement culture
description: Build a culture of continuous performance improvement using A/B testing, feature experimentation, and iterative optimization loops. Use caching, CDN, and in-memory stores to accelerate common access paths.

title: Load test at expected peak volumes
description: Validate workload performance at realistic peak load conditions. Simulate expected concurrency levels and traffic spikes before they reach production to surface degradation and identify scale limits.

title: Establish financial guardrails and policies
description: Define budgets, spending limits, and cost governance policies. Use Azure Cost Management and Azure Policy to enforce financial guardrails and prevent uncontrolled spending.

title: Align workload costs with business value
description: Map workload costs to the business outcomes they enable. Ensure every significant cost investment delivers proportional business value and establish a cost-to-value accountability model.

title: Collect and analyze cost data
description: Implement cost attribution through mandatory resource tagging. Use Azure Cost Management to analyze spending patterns, identify cost drivers, and detect unexpected charges.

title: Set spending guardrails
description: Configure budget alerts and spending thresholds to trigger notifications and automated responses when costs exceed defined limits. Use Azure Advisor cost recommendations proactively.

title: Dynamically allocate resources
description: Scale resources based on actual demand using autoscaling, scheduled scaling, and spot/preemptible instances. Avoid maintaining idle capacity and match provisioned resources to real-time workload requirements.

title: Rightsize assets
description: Match resource SKUs, tiers, and capacities to actual utilization. Use Azure Advisor rightsizing recommendations and utilization metrics to identify over-provisioned resources and downsize them.

title: Optimize component costs
description: Eliminate waste by removing unused resources such as unattached disks, idle public IPs, decommissioned load balancers, and orphaned snapshots. Automate cleanup with Azure Policy and lifecycle management.

title: Optimize environment costs
description: Apply differentiated cost strategies by environment type. Use lower SKU tiers for dev/test environments, shut down non-production resources outside working hours, and share infrastructure where safe.

title: Optimize personnel time
description: Reduce operational overhead by automating repetitive tasks and adopting managed services. Minimize manual toil through infrastructure as code, automation runbooks, and self-service platforms.

title: Optimize workload costs over time
description: Continuously review and optimize workload costs using lifecycle management policies, sunset unused features, and track cost trends. Apply storage lifecycle policies to move data to appropriate tiers.

title: Use flexible pricing models
description: Leverage Azure Reserved Instances, Savings Plans, and Azure Hybrid Benefit for predictable workloads. Match the pricing model to the consumption pattern to maximize discount rates.

title: Review and streamline cloud bills
description: Regularly review Azure invoices for unexpected charges, pricing model mismatches, and optimization opportunities. Use Azure Cost Management + Billing and Cost Analysis to track spending trends.
